title: Noghri revolution
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Quote|It looks like the ''kholm''-grass you remember, and even smells very much like it. But not exactly. It is, in fact, a subtle creation of the Empire: sent by the Emperor to poison your world.|Leia Organa Solo|<ref name="Dark Force Rising">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Rukh.jpg|thumb|The death of [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] at the hands of [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]]|270px|right]]
The '''Noghri revolution''', which occurred in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], was an uprising of the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]]. The Noghri people, who had been tricked into serving the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], had learned that they had been duped, thanks to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. In turn, they renounced their oath of Imperial service, and later helped the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Their main contribution was that made by [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]], the personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], who killed Thrawn over [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]].

==History==
===Prelude===
[[File:Trihexalophine1138.jpg|thumb|left|180px|250px|1138 poisons [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]] soil.]]
In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] ship crashed to the surface of [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]] after a [[Battle of Honoghr/Legends|battle over the Noghri world]]. The ship emitted [[Trihexalophine1138]], a poison which nearly destroyed the planet's flora.<ref name="Star Wars Republic 68: Armor">[[Republic 68|''Republic'' 68]]</ref> Only the [[kholm-grass]] could survive. The Noghri were unable to do anything about this, and many died. The race remained unknown to most of the galaxy's citizens.<ref name="Dark Force Rising" />

===Time of slavery===
Later after the rise of the Galactic Empire, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] came to the planet Honoghr and offered his help, planning to make the Noghri, who were skilled assassins, warriors for the Empire. He sent [[Decon III|decon droids]] to clean the planet. The Noghri thanked him and the Empire by putting a debt on themselves, and became Vader's private assassins, known as Death Commandos. Later on Vader introduced [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] to the Noghri as a reward for service to the Empire, and named him the new master of the Noghri.

===Exposure to the galaxy===
The Noghri served the Admiral, working as skilled assassins and warriors. One of them, Rukh, even became his bodyguard, traveling with him everywhere. Thrawn trusted the Noghri somewhat, as they thought he was a god. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Thrawn began [[Thrawn campaign|his campaign]] to destroy the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and become the new [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]]. Using the Noghri as one of his many components, he sent them to capture [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. However, they failed, one of the few times they did not accomplish their goal.<ref name="Heir to the Empire">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref>
[[File:Noghrismell.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Khabarakh recognizes Leia]]

Later, after he saw through Han Solo's plan to hide his wife on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to keep her safe, Thrawn sent Noghri to capture her. With the help of [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and other [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], she defeated them—the first time. Several nights later, more came, and fought their way into her hut. A Noghri named [[Khabarakh]] had her in his grasp when he stopped, smelling her for the first time. He had recognized her as ''[[Mal'ary'ush]]'', the daughter of Vader and his heir.<ref name="Heir to the Empire" />

===The revolution===
He agreed to meet Leia over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] later. The two then headed for Honoghr, as he thought she could help them. However, Thrawn was visiting Honoghr on the very same day, and recognized Khabarakh as the last survivor from the [[Imperial Noghri Commando Team 22|Commando Team 22]], which had been sent to Kashyyyk to capture Leia. Thrawn personally set off to the [[dukha]] of the [[clan Kihm'bar]] to interrogate Khabarakh about the events on Kashyyyk. Meanwhile Leia and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] were hidden in the dukha in order to not to be noticed by the Admiral. Thrawn sentenced Khabarakh with betrayal and sent him to [[Nystao]] to humiliate him in public.<ref name="Dark Force Rising" />

[[File:Honoghr_Leia.jpg|thumb|left|260px|Leia arrives at Honoghr]]

Leia, Chewie and the maitrakh planned to set Khabarakh free when the moon was the farthest from the planet, and managed to. Leia later learned from the maitrakh that the devastation had not been during the Galactic Civil War as she thought, but decades earlier. She realized that the Empire had tricked them. Later, she learned of the complete duplicity of their plan when she examined the kholm-grass and proved that the decon droids were essentially doing nothing: they were actually killing mutated kholm-grass planted by the Empire at a very slow rate. After that, the Noghri realized they had been fooled, and swore vengeance. They turned against Thrawn, but secretly. The Noghri vowed to serve Leia until their debt was repaid.<ref name="Dark Force Rising" />

Thrawn began to suspect something was amiss with his loyal servants, but was unaware of exactly what was troubling them.<ref name="The Last Command">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>

===The death of Thrawn===
[[File:Thrawn_Dead_EC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Grand Admiral Thrawn dies after being stabbed by Rukh]]
Not long afterward, Thrawn was killed during the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]] by his bodyguard Rukh, setting the Noghri free, and ending the crisis that was coming close to destroying the New Republic. The Empire had suffered a grievous blow, and would never recover from the death of their last hope.<ref name="The Last Command" />

===Aftermath===
The Noghri became part of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. They continued to serve as bodyguards for Leia and her family. Though Rukh had been killed by [[Grodin Tierce (clone)|Grodin Tierce]] onboard the [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], he became a hero for setting the Noghri free. The Noghri vowed to fight against the Empire, and tried to forget their years of service, taking it as a personal insult if someone mentioned it.<ref name="Specter of the Past">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>

==Appearances==
